name: Multivariate Gaussian Cluster Analysis
description:
This interactive scatter plot demonstrates multivariate Gaussian distributions, commonly used in machine learning for cluster analysis and anomaly detection. Each point represents an individual observation sampled from a distinct probability distribution.

  • Clusters: The grouping of points indicates a high density of occurrences around a central mean (centroid).
  • Variance: The 'spread' of each cluster represents the standard deviation of the underlying data; wider clusters indicate higher variance.
  • Overlapping Regions: Areas where different colored points mix suggest regions of uncertainty where a classification model might struggle to distinguish between classes.

Scatter plots are the primary tool for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA), allowing researchers to visually identify correlations, trends, and outliers before applying algorithmic processing.
